questions.question: A pipeline stage fetches an instruction from memory and decodes it into its instruction opcode, operand fetch, and control signals. Which of the following stages is responsible for executing the instruction opcode?
A complete instruction consists of the following components: opcode, operands, and control signals. Which of the following is NOT a component of a complete instruction?

A pipelined processor has 5 stages: instruction fetch, decode, operand fetch, execution, and memory access. Which stage is responsible for performing the actual computation required by the instruction?
